One-third of the classrooms have 30 desks, so that's 15 * (1/3) = 5 classrooms with 30 desks each, which is 5 * 30 = 150 desks. The rest of the classrooms have 25 desks each, so that's 15 - 5 = 10 classrooms with 25 desks each, which is 10 * 25 = 250 desks. In total, there are 150 + 250 = 400 desks. thought: John's age 11 years ago can be found by subtracting 11 from his current age. Let's call that A. In 9 years, his age will be A + 9. At that time, he will be 3 times as old as he was 11 years ago, so we can write the equation: A + 9 = 3*(A - 11). Solving for A will give us his current age.
Solving the equation A + 9 = 3*(A - 11) for A gives us A = (9 + 11 * 3) / 2.
